title: elymus repens subsp. elongatiformis
intro: Elymus repens subsp. elongatiformis, also known as the creeping bentgrass or quaking grass, is a subspecies of the Elymus repens species, a type of grass native to Europe and Asia. The medicinal properties of Elymus repens subsp. elongatiformis lie in its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which make it a potential natural remedy for various health issues. The health benefits associated with this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Elymus repens subsp. elongatiformis is prepared as a tea or infusion, and its dried roots and leaves are sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create medicinal preparations.
